title: Draba obconica
intro: Draba obconica, also known as Alpine Whitlow Grass, is a small, annual plant that belongs to the family Brassicaceae. It has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Draba obconica include isothiocyanates, which are responsible for its medicinal properties, and have been shown to have antimicrobial and anticancer effects. Draba obconica is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Draba obconica is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of the southwestern United States. The classification of Draba obconica is further divided into the genus Draba and the subgenus Draba, which is part of the Brassicaceae family. Within the genus Draba, Draba obconica is placed in the Draba sect. Drabopsis. The species Draba obconica is also known as the conic draba or obconic draba.
